monday.notes: Asia opens first (user's morning). US Monday opens late user evening. Typically range-setting day after weekend gap. Watch for weekend gap fills in first 2-3 hours.
monday.user_schedule: Active morning AEST. US open ~11:30PM AEST. Usually awake for US open.

tuesday.notes: Typically heaviest volume day of the week. API/EIA inventory data days (alternating). Good day for position building — liquidity supports larger size.
tuesday.user_schedule: Active morning AEST. US session runs into user's late evening/early morning.

wednesday.notes: EIA inventory report day (10:30 ET). FOMC announcements (when scheduled) at 14:00 ET. Mid-week — institutional rebalancing. Often highest volatility day for oil.
wednesday.user_schedule: EIA at 10:30 ET = ~12:30AM AEST Thu — user likely sleeping or catching late.

thursday.notes: Post-EIA positioning. ECB decisions (when scheduled). Weekly jobless claims 08:30 ET. Options expiry activity if monthly.
thursday.user_schedule: Normal AEST day. US session is user's late evening.

friday.notes: Position squaring before weekend. Volume drops after 14:00 ET. NFP first Friday of month at 08:30 ET. REDUCE LEVERAGE by 4PM ET — weekend liquidity thin, stop hunts common.
friday.user_schedule: User's Friday evening — may want to wind down. CRITICAL: reduce weekend exposure before 4PM ET.

saturday.notes: Market technically open but extremely thin. Stop hunts, wicks, manipulated moves. DO NOT add positions. Monitor only. Reduce leverage if haven't already.
saturday.user_schedule: User's weekend. Likely active morning AEST but should not be trading.

sunday.notes: Market reopens 6PM ET = Monday 8AM AEST. Asia session starts building. Watch for gap opens driven by weekend news. Good time for user to review week ahead and set thesis.
sunday.user_schedule: User's Monday morning AEST. Fresh start. Good time for weekly review.
